name: Lamb Börek
instructions:
1. Heat olive oil in a saucepan over medium-high heat. Add onion, salt, and lamb. Break up lamb into small crumbles and cook until most liquid evaporates, about 8 minutes.

2. Add garlic, currants, pine nuts, cumin, coriander, cinnamon, paprika, black pepper, cayenne, and allspice. Cook for 1 minute.

3. Add tomato sauce and water, stir, and reduce heat to medium. Cook until mixture dries, 20 to 30 minutes. Cool completely.

4. Combine egg, yogurt, water, and butter in a bowl and whisk together thoroughly.

5. Preheat oven to 400 degrees F (200 degrees C). Butter a baking pan.

6. Place 2 phyllo sheets on work surface, brushing each with egg wash. Repeat layering two more times.

7. Line half the lamb filling along one edge of the phyllo, roll up, and place in baking pan. Repeat with remaining filling and phyllo.

8. Brush top with egg wash, sprinkle with sesame seeds, and bake until browned, 35 to 40 minutes. Let cool for 15 minutes.

9. Combine yogurt, mint, lemon juice, and garlic for the sauce. Adjust consistency with water and season with salt and cayenne.

10. Cut Börek into wedges and serve with yogurt sauce.
simpleInstructions:
1. Cook onion and lamb, then add spices and tomato sauce. Cook until dry.

2. Prepare egg wash and preheat the oven.

3. Layer phyllo sheets with egg wash and fill with lamb mixture.

4. Roll, brush with egg wash, sprinkle sesame seeds, bake, and cool.

5. Mix yogurt sauce and serve.
